question:
A Three-unit Load-Sharing System
A shared-load system refers to a parallel system whose units equally share the system function. Therefore, for a shared-load parallel system to fail, all the units in the system must fail. Determine the reliability and mean time to failure (MTTF) of a 3-unit shared-load parallel system where
$\lambda_0$ is the constant failure rate of a unit when all the three units are operational;
$\lambda_h$ is the constant failure rate of each of the two surviving units, each of which shares half of the total load; and.
$\lambda_f \quad$ is the constant failure rate of a unit at full load.
(a) Determine the reliability at $t=10 \mathrm{~h}$ where

$$
\lambda_0=0.001 / \text { hour } ; \lambda_h=0.005 / \text { hour } ; \text { and } \lambda_f=0.05 / \text { hour } .
$$

(b) What is the MTTF?
answer:
Consider the following three events:
Event 1: All the three units are working until the end of the mission time t where each unit shares one-third of the total load.
Event 2: All the three units are working until time $t_1$ (each shares one-third of the total load). At time $t_1$, one of the units (say unit 1) fails, and the other two units (say unit 2 and 3) remain to work until the mission time t. Here, once a unit fails at time $t_1$, the remaining two working units would take half each of the total load and have a constant rate $\lambda_h$. As for all identical units, there are 3 possibilities under this situation.
Event 3: All the three units are working until time $t_1$ (each shares one-third of the total load) when one (say unit 1) of the three units fails. At time $t_2,\left(t_2>t_1\right)$ one more unit fails (say unit 2) and the remaining unit works until the end of the mission time $t$. Under this event, there are 6 possibilities that the probability of two units failing before time $t$ and only one unit remains to work until time $t$.
Let $\mathrm{T}_1, \mathrm{~T}_2$ and $\mathrm{T}_3$ be failure time of unit 1,2 and 3 , respectively. Let
$\mathrm{R}_0(\mathrm{t})$ be the reliability of a unit when all the three units are operational $R_h(t)$ be reliability of each of the two remaining working units each of which shares half of the total load
$R_f(t)$ be the reliability of a unit at full load.
Then

$$
\begin{aligned}
P_1(t) & \equiv P\{\text { Event } 1\}=P\left\{T_1>t, T_2>t, T_3>t\right\} \\
& =\left(R_0(t)\right)^3 \\
& =\mathrm{e}^{-3 \lambda_0 \mathrm{t}}
\end{aligned}
$$


Next,

$$
\begin{aligned}
P_2(t) & \equiv P\{\text { Event } 2\} \\
& =3 P\left\{\left\{T_1=t_1<t, T_2>t_1, T_3>t_1\right\} \text { and }\left\{T_2>\left(t-t_1\right) \text { and } T_3>\left(t-t_1\right)\right\}\right\}+ \\
& =3 \int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{f}_0\left(t_1\right) R_0^2\left(t_1\right) R_h^2\left(t-t_1\right) d t_1
\end{aligned}
$$
where $f_0\left(\mathrm{t}_1\right)$ is the pdf of a unit with one-third of the total load when all three units are functioning. For exponential distribution function, from Eq. (4.62) we have

$$
\begin{aligned}
P_2(t) & =3 \int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \lambda_0 \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_0 t_1} \mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_0 t_1} \mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_h\left(t-t_1\right)} d t_1 \\
& =\frac{3 \lambda_0}{2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0}\left(\mathrm{e}^{-3 \lambda_0 t}-\mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_h t}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$


Finally,

$$
\begin{aligned}
P_3(t) & \equiv P\{\text { Event 3 }\} \\
& =6 P\left\{\left\{T_1=\mathrm{t}_1<t, T_2>t_1, T_3>t_1\right\} \cap\left\{T_2=t_2>t_1 \text { and } \mathrm{T}_3>t_2\right\} \cap\left\{T_3>\left(t-t_1-t_2\right)\right\}\right\} \\
& =6 \int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{f}_0\left(t_1\right) R_0^2\left(t_1\right)\left\{\int_0^{\mathrm{t}-\mathrm{t}_1} \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{h}}\left(t_2\right) R_h\left(t_2\right) R_f\left(t-t_1-t_2\right) d t_2\right\} d t_1
\end{aligned}
$$

where $f_h\left(t_2\right)$ is the pdf of a unit with a half of the total load.
For exponential distribution function, from Eq. (4.64) we obtain

$$
P_3(t)=6 \int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \lambda_0 \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_0 t_1} \mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_0 t_1}\left\{\int_0^{t-t_1} \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t_2} \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t_2} \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{f}}\left(t-t_1-t_2\right)} d t_2\right\} d t_1
$$
$$
=6 \lambda_0 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-3 \lambda_0 t}}{\left(3 \lambda_0-2 \lambda_h\right)\left(3 \lambda_0-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t}}{\left(2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(2 \lambda_h-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{e^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{f}} t}}{\left(\lambda_f-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(\lambda_f-2 \lambda_h\right)}\right]
$$


Note that one can also calculate the $\mathrm{P}_3(\mathrm{t})$ as follows:

$$
\begin{aligned}
P_3(t) & \equiv P\{\text { Event } 3\} \\
& =\binom{3}{1}\binom{2}{1} P\left\{\left\{T_1=\mathrm{t}_1<t, T_2>t_1, T_3>t_1\right\} \cap\left\{T_2=t_2-t_1 \& \mathrm{~T}_3>t_2-t_1\right\} \cap\left\{T_3>\left(t-t_2\right)\right\}\right\} \\
& =6 \int_{\mathrm{t}_1=0}^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{f}_0\left(t_1\right) R_0^2\left(t_1\right)\left\{\int_{\mathrm{t}_2=\mathrm{t}_1}^{\mathrm{t}} \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{h}}\left(t_2-t_1\right) R_h\left(t_2-t_1\right) R_f\left(t-t_2\right) d t_2\right\} d t_1
\end{aligned}
$$

which is the same as Eq. (4.64). For exponential distribution function, from Eq. (4.66) we obtain as follows:

$$
\begin{aligned}
P_3(t) & =6 \int_0^{\mathrm{t}} \lambda_0 \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_0 t_1} \mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_0 t_1}\left\{\int_{t_2=t_1}^t \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{h}}\left(t_2-t_1\right)} \mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{h}}\left(t_2-t_1\right)} e^{-\lambda_f\left(t-t_2\right)} d t_2\right\} d t_1 \\
& =6 \lambda_0 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}}\left[\frac{e^{-3 \lambda_0 t}}{\left(3 \lambda_0-2 \lambda_h\right)\left(3 \lambda_0-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{e^{-2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t}}{\left(2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(2 \lambda_h-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{e^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{f}} t}}{\left(\lambda_f-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(\lambda_f-2 \lambda_h\right)}\right] \\
& =\frac{6 \lambda_0 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}}}{\left(\lambda_f-2 \lambda_h\right)}\left[\frac{e^{-3 \lambda_0 t}}{\left(2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0\right)}-\frac{e^{-2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t}}{\left(2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0\right)}-\frac{e^{-3 \lambda_0 t}}{\left(\lambda_f-3 \lambda_0\right)}+\frac{e^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{f}} t}}{\left(\lambda_f-3 \lambda_0\right)}\right]
\end{aligned}
$$

where Eq. (4.67) is the same as Eq. (4.65). Thus, the reliability of a three-unit shared load parallel system is
$$
\begin{aligned}
R(t)= & \mathrm{P}_1(t)+\mathrm{P}_2(t)+\mathrm{P}_3(t) \\
= & \mathrm{e}^{-3 \lambda_0 t}+\frac{3 \lambda_0}{2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0}\left(e^{-3 \lambda_0 t}-\mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t}\right) \\
& +6 \lambda_0 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}}\left[\frac{e^{-3 \lambda_0 t}}{\left(3 \lambda_0-2 \lambda_h\right)\left(3 \lambda_0-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{e^{-2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t}}{\left(2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(2 \lambda_h-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{e^{-\lambda_{\mathrm{f}} t}}{\left(\lambda_f-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(\lambda_f-2 \lambda_h\right)}\right] .
\end{aligned}
$$


The system mean time to failure (MTTF) is

$$
\begin{aligned}
\text { MTTF } & =\int_0^{\infty} R(t) d t \\
& =\int_0^{\infty}\left\{\begin{array}{l}
\mathrm{e}^{-3 \lambda_0 t}+\frac{3 \lambda_0}{2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0}\left(\mathrm{e}^{-3 \lambda_0 t}-e^{-2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t}\right) \\
\left.+6 \lambda_0 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}}\left[\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-3 \lambda_0 t}}{\left(3 \lambda_0-2 \lambda_h\right)\left(3 \lambda_0-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}} t}}{\left(2 \lambda_h-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(2 \lambda_h-\lambda_f\right)}+\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-\lambda_f t}}{\left(\lambda_f-3 \lambda_0\right)\left(\lambda_f-2 \lambda_h\right)}\right]\right\} d t \\
\\
\\
=\frac{1}{3 \lambda_0}+\frac{1}{2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}}}+\frac{1}{\lambda_{\mathrm{f}}}
\end{array}\right.
\end{aligned}
$$


Given $\lambda_0=0.001 /$ hour $; \quad \lambda_h=0.005 /$ hour $;$ and $\lambda_f=0.05 /$ hour . From Eq. (4.68), the reliability at time $\mathrm{t}=10 \mathrm{~h}$ is given by
$$
\begin{aligned}
&\begin{aligned}
R(t)= & \mathrm{e}^{-3(0.001)(10)}+\frac{3(0.001)}{2(0.005)-3(0.001)}\left(\mathrm{e}^{-3(0.001)(10)}-\mathrm{e}^{-2(0.005)(10)}\right) \\
& +6(0.001)(0.005)\left[\begin{array}{l}
\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-3(0.001)(10)}}{(3(0.001)-2(0.005)(3(0.001)-0.05)} \\
+\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-2(0.005)(10)}}{(2(0.005)-3(0.001))(2(0.005)-0.05)} \\
+\frac{\mathrm{e}^{-(0.05)(10)}}{(0.05-3(0.001))(0.05-2(0.005))}
\end{array}\right] \\
= & 0.9997 .
\end{aligned}\\
&\text { The system mean time to failure (MTTF) is }\\
&\begin{aligned}
\text { MTTF } & =\frac{1}{3 \lambda_0}+\frac{1}{2 \lambda_{\mathrm{h}}}+\frac{1}{\lambda_{\mathrm{f}}} \\
& =\frac{1}{3(0.001)}+\frac{1}{2(0.005)}+\frac{1}{(0.05)} \\
& =453.33 \text { hours. }
\end{aligned}
\end{aligned}
$$
